title: Coil Rating Parameters
summary: Standard rating parameters for extended-surface air-cooling and dehumidifying coils per AHRI Standard 410.

title: Coil Rating Parameters
summary: Standard rating parameters for extended-surface air-cooling and dehumidifying coils per AHRI Standard 410.
evidence_quote: Entering air wet-bulb temperature: 60 to 85°F (if air is not dehumidified in the application, select coils based on sensible heat transfer)

title: Coil Rating Parameters
summary: Standard rating parameters for extended-surface air-cooling and dehumidifying coils per AHRI Standard 410.

title: Coil Rating Parameters
summary: Standard rating parameters for extended-surface air-cooling and dehumidifying coils per AHRI Standard 410.
evidence_quote: Evaporator refrigerant saturation temperature: 30 to 55°F at coil suction outlet (refrigerant vapor superheat at coil suction outlet is 6°F or higher)

title: Coil Rating Parameters
summary: Standard rating parameters for extended-surface air-cooling and dehumidifying coils per AHRI Standard 410.

title: Coil Rating Parameters
summary: Standard rating parameters for extended-surface air-cooling and dehumidifying coils per AHRI Standard 410.

title: Coil Sensible Heat Ratio Range
summary: Typical range of sensible to total heat ratio for dehumidifying coils in practice.
evidence_quote: The air-side ratio of sensible to total heat removed by dehumidifying coils varies in practice from about 0.6 to 1.0 (i.e., sensible heat is from 60 to 100% of the total, depending on the application).

title: Typical Coil Air Face Velocity
summary: Typical air face velocity range for selecting dehumidifying coils for comfort applications.
evidence_quote: Dehumidifying coils for comfort applications are frequently selected in the range of 400 to 500 fpm air face velocity.

title: HTW Generator Safety Controls
summary: Safety control requirement for high-temperature water (HTW) generators to prevent tube failure.
evidence_quote: HTW generators require a minimum water flow at all times to prevent tube failure.

title: HTW Control Valve Sizing
summary: Sizing criterion for control valves in high-temperature water (HTW) systems.
evidence_quote: The valve and equipment must be sized to absorb, in the control valve at full flow, not less than half the available pressure difference between supply and return mains where the equipment is served.

title: Ceiling Plenum Pressure Issue
summary: Potential problem with ceiling plenum applications in high-rise buildings due to stack effect.
evidence_quote: Some ceiling plenum applications with lay-in panels do not work well where the stack effect of a high-rise building or high-rise elevators creates a negative pressure.

title: Return Air Plenum Temperature Difference
summary: Typical temperature difference in a return air plenum directly below a roof deck during the air-conditioning season.
evidence_quote: The air temperature in a return air plenum directly below a roof deck is usually higher by 3 to 5°F during the air-conditioning season than in a ducted return.

title: Underfloor Air Distribution (UFAD) Critical Factor
summary: Critical success factor for underfloor air distribution (UFAD) systems.
evidence_quote: Making the underfloor supply air plenum as airtight as possible is critical to the success of this type of plenum.

title: Pipe vs. Duct Space Requirement
summary: Space requirement comparison between pipe distribution and air ductwork.
evidence_quote: When assessing application of pipe distribution to air distribution, the floor-to-floor height requirement can influence the pipe system: it requires less ceiling space to install pipe when compared to air ductwork.

title: Enhanced Filtration for Security
summary: Level of enhanced particulate filtration recommended for air-handling systems in buildings with security concerns.
evidence_quote: Enhanced particulate filtration for air-handling systems to the level of MERV 14 to 16 filters not only tends to reduce circulation of dangerous substances (e.g., anthrax), but also provides better indoor air quality (IAQ).
